no-restricted-globals.name: app
no-restricted-globals.message: Avoid using the global app object. Instead use the reference provided by your plugin instance.

no-restricted-globals.name: fetch
no-restricted-globals.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `fetch` for network requests in Obsidian.

no-restricted-globals.name: localStorage
no-restricted-globals.message: Prefer `App#saveLocalStorage` / `App#loadLocalStorage` functions to write / read localStorage data that's unique to a vault.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: axios
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `axios`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: superagent
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `superagent`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: got
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `got`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: ofetch
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `ofetch`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: ky
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `ky`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: node-fetch
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: Use the built-in `requestUrl` function instead of `node-fetch`.

@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.name: moment
@typescript-eslint/no-restricted-imports.message: The 'moment' package is bundled with Obsidian. Import the `moment` value from 'obsidian' instead. For the `Moment` type, rely on the global `moment` namespace or add `type Moment = moment.Moment;` (type-only imports from 'moment' are allowed).

messages.Unexpected console statement. Only these: Avoid unnecessary logging to console. See https://docs.obsidian.md/Plugins/Releasing/Plugin+guidelines#Avoid+unnecessary+logging+to+console

messages.The Function constructor is eval: Using the `Function` constructor is dangerous because it executes arbitrary code, similar to `eval()`
